business.geography_hint: Netherlands (Amsterdam HQ, Breezanddijk R&D; Delft/Wetsus origin)

engine.ladder_reasoning: Visitor is evaluating a TECHNOLOGY/product (electrochemical Direct Ocean Capture and its two output products, CDR credits and green CO2): the site argues via mechanism, features, how-it-works, MRV verification and scaling vision. Not selling a named person's expertise, not a portfolio, not a browsable place, and the offer is not pre-understood.

goal.one_action: book a call to buy carbon-removal credits / order green CO2 (secondary: subscribe to newsletter, become a partner, apply to careers)

structure.archetype_note: Deep-tech B2B company site: home + tech deep-dives (technology, MRV) + about/team + projects/map + blog/careers. Conversion diffused across several book-a-call and subscribe CTAs rather than one action.

mapped.observed_as: Giga-ton scalable Carbon Dioxide Removal Technology
HOW IT WORKS / Our Direct Ocean Capture process
'It is easier to extract CO2 from water than from the air.' — David Vermaas, Scientific Co-founder
Meet the Team (~20 staff, LinkedIn) + Our Partners
MRV page (monitoring, reporting & verification of removals, LCA, storage durability)

unmapped.observed: Sites & Projects (project locations gallery + office locations on Google Maps)
unmapped.why_no_fit: Mixes a project-showcase gallery with an office-locations map; neither the deployment-sites showcase nor the dual R&D/office map is a single canonical section
A standalone third-party-verifiability/measurement-credibility deep page; 'security' is the nearest but understates a carbon-integrity proof page

proposed_new.name: blog-teaser
proposed_new.evidence: 'Our latest news' strip on home shows recent posts linking into the News blog (reused canonical slug)

confidence.why: fetch_quality=full and structure is clear, but the conversion goal is genuinely diffuse — Calendly (buy credits / order CO2), a separate Google Calendar partner scheduler, newsletter subscribe, and careers all compete, so 'one_action' is a judgement call rather than obvious.

gap_findings: Multi-offer B2B deep-tech company: two product pathways (CDR credit sales vs green-CO2 ordering) each with its own book-a-call CTA, plus partner scheduling, newsletter, and careers — engines assume ONE conversion action; here 4-5 compete with no single dominant one.
Two different scheduling backends coexist (Calendly for buyers, Google Calendar appointments for partners) — 'delegated_to' can only name one.
MRV / carbon-integrity verification is a load-bearing proof page for cleantech buyers that no copy engine or canonical section names (mapped to 'security' as nearest fit).
